Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Defendant?S Discovery Requests
Off Calendar Feb-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Diallah Bhanji, Esq. Bhanji Law Firm 425-281-6858 for Plaintiff.
Adam Shapiro. Esq. Deputy City Attorney 415-554-4284 Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

ARGUED. Ms. Nhanji contests Special Interrogatory 51 nad RFP 42. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTs EDWARD OCHI,and DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Defendant?S Discovery Requests.

Defendants' motion to compel further discovery responses is granted as to requests for admissions 9-10, special interrogatory 51, and request for production 42 and denied as to requests for admission 17 and request for production 41.

Requests for admissions 9-10, special interrogatory 51, and request for production 42 are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Defendants have shown that they met and conferred in a reasonable and good faith manner and plaintiff's responses are incomplete. (Shapiro Dec., pars. 28-31, 34.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated that any of his objections has merit. Plaintiff must provide further responses no later than March 10, 2025.

Request for admission 17 is moot. This request relates to the plaintiff's FEHA retaliation cause of action. Summary adjudication was granted on that claim on February 24, 2025. Request for production 41 seeks "Any notes, diaries, logs, journals, letters, electronic mail, text messages, calendars, Facebook postings, tweets, or other social media messages that relate or refer to your employment with the CITY, your alleged familial insomnia, or your activities on days when you requested FMLA leave." The request is overly broad and burdensome. As phrased, the request seeks production of all documents that "relate or refer to" plaintiff's employment.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
